title: dimocarpus fumatus subsp. philippinensis
intro: Dimocarpus fumatus subsp. philippinensis, also known as the Philippine fruit, is a plant species that belongs to the Sapindaceae family and is native to the Philippines. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including its ability to treat fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions due to its antibacterial, anti-inflammatory, and antifungal properties. The health benefits of Dimocarpus fumatus subsp. philippinensis include its potential to reduce fever, alleviate pain, and improve skin health, as well as its ability to act as an antioxidant and anti-inflammatory agent. The plant is traditionally prepared as a herbal tea, infusion, or tincture, and is also used in folk remedies to treat various ailments, making it a valuable resource in traditional medicine.
